name: Eva Jessye
abstract: Eva Jessye (January 20, 1895 – February 21, 1992) was an American conductor and composer who was the first black woman to receive international distinction as a professional choral conductor. She is notable as a choral conductor during the Harlem Renaissance.

works.t: Wheel, in a Wheel, in a Wheel

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: what's her name -- Eva Jessy, she had the choir, Eva Jessy's choir.
